$schema_note: Source of truth for what Keystone builds. Human views: ROADMAP.md (phases) and TASKS.md. Validated by scripts/validate_feature_list.py (run in pytest + CI). Phases 2–5 are aligned to the three compliance layers (build order = phase number; each phase names its layer) per ADR-0011. Each item carries a 'layer' tag. Do not invent scope beyond the roadmap.
rules: id matches KS-NNNN and is unique
id follows KS-0Pnn where P is the phase number
done_criteria is a non-empty list for every item (state how we'll know it's done)
tests must be non-empty once status passes 'planned' (in_progress | done)
every test ref 'path' or 'path::node' must resolve to a real file (and node) in tests/
depends_on (optional) is a list of existing feature ids — a structural prerequisite edge, not just an implied ordering

features.title: src-layout package importable, versioned, typed
features.description: src/keystone/ imports cleanly, exposes __version__, ships py.typed.
done_criteria: import keystone succeeds
keystone.__version__ is a non-empty str
py.typed is shipped in the package

features.title: Console entry point reports version
features.description: keystone.__main__:main prints the version (placeholder CLI).
done_criteria: main() prints a line containing __version__
the installed console script, run as a subprocess, prints the version

features.title: Strict quality gates enforced
features.description: ruff (incl. security S rules), mypy strict, and a blocking coverage floor run as one gate (make check / CI check job).
done_criteria: make check runs ruff + mypy + fast tests + audit and exits 0
coverage below the floor fails the build

features.title: Secret/hygiene hooks enforced in CI
features.description: pre-commit (detect-secrets + hygiene) runs as its own CI gate, not only locally.
done_criteria: ci.yml has a pre-commit job running on push/PR
detect-secrets uses a committed baseline

features.title: Progressive-disclosure docs
features.description: Thin CLAUDE.md map + structured, cross-linked docs/ tree; no orphans.
done_criteria: CLAUDE.md is <= 120 lines and links to every governance doc
every docs/*.md is reachable and has no broken relative links

features.title: Deterministic-core import boundary enforced
features.description: import-linter contract: the deterministic core must not import the agent/guardrails/LLM-edge layers. Wired into pre-commit + CI.
done_criteria: import-linter contract exists in pyproject.toml
lint-imports passes against the materialized layer packages
the contract runs in pre-commit and CI

features.title: NAT YAML workflow skeleton
features.description: NeMo Agent Toolkit workflow definition as the orchestration entry point.
done_criteria: a NAT workflow YAML loads/validates
an entry point can resolve and invoke the workflow

features.title: Hash-chained evidence ledger — append
features.description: Append-only SQLite ledger where each entry commits to the prior entry's hash.
done_criteria: appending an event stores a row whose hash includes the previous hash
the first entry chains from a defined genesis value

features.title: Evidence ledger — tamper detection
features.description: Chain verification detects any modification/insertion/deletion.
done_criteria: verify() returns true for an intact chain
verify() returns false when any entry is altered

features.title: Inference config switch (NIM / Ollama)
features.description: One code path; configuration selects hosted NIM (demo) vs local Ollama (production).
done_criteria: config selects the backend without branching the code path
an invalid backend value is rejected with a clear error

features.title: Curated obligation graph with per-node source citations
features.description: ~25–30 obligation nodes spanning EU AI Act Art. 9–15, DORA, India DPDP Act + DPDP Rules 2025, RBI responsible-AI guidance, and PMLA/FIU-IND. Each node carries a source-citation field (instrument + article/section). Re-scoped from the former generic 'compliance agent' (old KS-0201).
done_criteria: the graph has 25–30 nodes covering EU AI Act Art. 9–15, DORA, India DPDP Act + DPDP Rules 2025, RBI responsible-AI, and PMLA/FIU-IND, and every node carries a non-empty source citation (instrument + article/section)

features.title: Deterministic crosswalk/dedup onto a shared control library
features.description: Deterministic mapping that crosswalks and de-duplicates obligations onto a shared control library keyed to ISO/IEC 42001 + FATF + NIST AI RMF. No LLM in this path (deterministic core).
done_criteria: obligations deterministically and reproducibly map onto the shared control library (ISO/IEC 42001 + FATF + NIST AI RMF) with duplicate controls collapsed
every non-empty control_id on an obligation resolves to a real control in the library; an unresolved reference fails the build (ADR-0012 §5b)

features.title: EU hard-law vs India self-certification modality contrast
features.description: The EU hard-law-conformity vs. India self-certification regulatory modality is an explicit, surfaced attribute on the relevant obligations — not buried in prose.
done_criteria: each affected obligation exposes an explicit modality attribute (hard-law-conformity | self-certification) that the output/UI surfaces

features.title: LLM-edge obligation-summary phrasing
features.description: The LLM is used ONLY to phrase human-readable obligation summaries; all graph/crosswalk/modality logic stays in the deterministic core (honors the core/edge boundary).
done_criteria: obligation summaries are produced at the LLM edge while the import-linter boundary keeps graph/crosswalk logic LLM-free

features.title: Citation-validation accuracy budget
features.description: A citation-validation test enforces the accuracy budget: every article/section reference is well-formed and sourced; the build fails on any unsourced or malformed node.
done_criteria: a validation test fails the build if any obligation node has a missing or malformed source citation

features.title: Deterministic deontic-strength guard for edge phrasing
features.description: The deontic strength (binding 'shall/must' vs advisory 'should/may') of an obligation is a deterministic-core fact. KS-0204's edge phrasing is guarded by a tiered (STRONG/MEDIUM/WEAK) classifier cross-checked against enforcement_modality: on any STRONG-strength transition (incl. a STRONG source whose phrasing can't be confirmed) or a HARD_LAW node phrased advisory, phrase_summary falls back to the authoritative curated summary (certainly-faithful over probably-faithful; never fails the build). Within-advisory drift off hard-law is an accepted, chosen scope line. Prevents a drifted verb being shown next to a binding/advisory modality label (KS-0203).
done_criteria: a deterministic tiered (STRONG/MEDIUM/WEAK) classifier in keystone.core, cross-checked against enforcement_modality, detects modal drift; phrase_summary falls back to the curated summary (never raises) on any STRONG-strength transition (including a STRONG source whose phrased force cannot be confirmed) or a HARD_LAW node phrased advisory

features.title: Tool-calling inference seam (complete_with_tools + cross-backend normalization)
features.description: Spike-discovered Phase-3 prerequisite (see the ADR-0011 amendment): plain complete() cannot tool-call, so keystone.llm.inference gains complete_with_tools(messages, tools) -> ToolCallResult. It sends the tools array to whichever backend is active and normalizes the cross-backend tool-call arguments difference the spike found — Ollama returns a JSON object, NIM (OpenAI-style) a JSON string — into one canonical typed shape (arguments ALWAYS a dict). Deterministic LLM-edge infrastructure; NO agent. KS-0301 (the mock agent) calls tools through this seam.
done_criteria: complete_with_tools returns a typed ToolCallResult whose tool-call arguments are ALWAYS a dict, regardless of backend
an Ollama-shaped response (arguments as a JSON object) and a NIM-shaped response (arguments as a JSON string) normalize to an identical result — backend parity
a text-only response yields an empty tool-call list (a valid outcome, not an error); malformed arguments raise a clear, typed error
the fast gate proves normalization with mocked HTTP (no Ollama running, no NIM key); a live tool-call per backend is slow and skips cleanly

features.title: Mock vulnerable payments agent
features.description: A deliberately vulnerable mock payments agent (keystone.assurance) backed by the KS-0300 tool-calling seam, susceptible to indirect prompt injection — the assurance loop's target surface. Vulnerable BY DESIGN: its system prompt + tool-wiring trust the transaction memo as instructions (OWASP ASI / EchoLeak-class), so a memo-injected settlement instruction drives an unauthorized initiate_transfer (a stub that records transfer intent to the evidence ledger). Ships the canonical, typed VulnerabilitySignature + malicious-memo example that the Garak probe (KS-0303) and the L1 fraud fixture (KS-0403) both import.
done_criteria: the mock agent runs end-to-end on synthetic input and is demonstrably susceptible to a defined indirect-prompt-injection vector (memo-instruction injection fires an unauthorized initiate_transfer)

the canonical, typed vulnerability signature + malicious-memo example are one importable source of truth (the L2↔L1 seam), with initiate_transfer a pure stub that only records transfer intent to the ledger

features.title: NeMo Guardrails rails (input/output/dialog)
features.description: A real NeMo Guardrails (v0.22) deterministic input rail wrapping the mock agent, closing the KS-0301 memo-injection hole: it vets the untrusted memo/data field before the agent acts and refuses embedded instructions, while leaving benign and legitimate transfers intact. Uses no main LLM and no embedding model (nothing downloaded). PROVEN by re-running the KS-0303 Garak detector against the guarded agent — the probe that failed 10/12 unguarded reports 0/12 guarded — and a 'vulnerability remediated' finding is written to the evidence ledger. Survives from old KS-0202 (re-phased). Built AFTER KS-0303 (ADR-0011 amendment): the rail's done-criteria needs the detector first.
done_criteria: a real NeMo Guardrails input rail blocks the memo-injection vector (the agent no longer fires an unauthorized initiate_transfer) while benign and legitimate transfers still succeed
re-running the KS-0303 Garak probe against the guarded agent goes clean (10/12 -> 0/12) and a vulnerability-remediated finding is written to the evidence ledger

features.title: Garak red-team as subprocess
features.description: Garak invoked as an isolated subprocess (ADR-0003, not a dependency) against the vulnerable mock agent (KS-0301), with its JSONL report parsed deterministically into typed findings, mapped to OWASP (LLM01 + Agentic ASI) → EU AI Act Art. 15 → India RBI principle, checked for the memo-injection signature, and written to the evidence ledger. Pinned to Garak 0.15.1; fast tests run over captured report fixtures, the live scan is -m slow. Built before KS-0302 so the detector exists before the patch it verifies (ADR-0011 amendment). Survives from old KS-0301 (renumbered within Phase 3).
done_criteria: a garak run is launched via subprocess against the mock agent and its report is captured
the parsed report is mapped to OWASP + EU/India regulation and the memo-injection vulnerability is detected and written to the ledger

features.title: Milestone: end-to-end AI assurance loop
features.description: A milestone-marked test proving the end-to-end Layer-2 loop, ORCHESTRATED BY THE NeMo Agent Toolkit: the existing pieces (KS-0301 agent, KS-0303 Garak detector + OWASP/regulation mapping, KS-0302 Guardrails patch) are composed into one arc — exposed → detected → mapped → patched → verified — each stage writing to the hash-chained evidence ledger. NAT's runtime drives the workflow (keystone_assurance_loop); `make milestone` runs it live (Garak fails 10/12 unguarded -> 0/12 guarded, exploit fires then does not). No new capability; just orchestration. Survives from old KS-0302.
done_criteria: a -m milestone test exercises the full assurance loop and asserts a verifiable, ordered, hash-valid ledger arc (exposed -> detected -> mapped -> patched -> verified)
the loop is orchestrated by the NeMo Agent Toolkit (a registered NAT workflow drives the stages), runnable live via `make milestone`

features.title: Transaction substrate: model + synthetic stream (Layer 1)
features.description: The deterministic Layer-1 data substrate: a typed Pydantic Transaction (id, timestamp, sender/recipient accounts, amount, currency, type, and a free-text memo with untrusted-data semantics) plus a deterministic, seeded synthetic generator. The generator produces mostly-normal traffic with the explicit, opt-in ability to seed FATF-detectable patterns (a structuring / rapid-movement cluster), labelling nothing as fraud. Shaped so the later seam (KS-0403) is possible: the memo is the same injection locus the Layer-2 agent trusted, and the structuring pattern is independently suspicious on financial-crime grounds. No detection (KS-0402) and no seam (KS-0403) yet. Re-scoped within Phase 4: the old monitor/scoring scope moves to KS-0402.
done_criteria: a typed Transaction model round-trips and fails loud on malformed / missing / bad-enum input, and carries an arbitrary free-text memo (the seam locus)
a deterministic seeded generator reproduces an identical stream and can emit a structuring / rapid-movement cluster on explicit request

features.title: FATF typology engine (Layer 1 detection)
features.description: Deterministic typology detection over the KS-0401 transaction stream: scores a structuring / rapid-movement pattern on financial-crime grounds and appends a verifiable finding to the evidence ledger. No LLM (deterministic core). Re-scoped within Phase 4: this is the detection/scoring the old KS-0401 monitor described; the planted-fraud-via-injection content folds into the KS-0403 seam.
done_criteria: the engine scores a synthetic transaction stream for a FATF structuring / rapid-movement typology and appends a verifiable finding to the ledger

features.title: L2↔L1 seam milestone (single owned item)
features.description: One owned task asserting the seam structurally: a planted fraudulent transfer enters the Transaction Monitor via the SAME indirect-prompt-injection path Garak flags against the mock payments agent (the memo carrying CANONICAL_MEMO_EXPLOIT / MEMO_INJECTION_SIGNATURE), AND is independently catchable as a FATF typology. The link is asserted structurally, so a refactor that decouples the two fails the build.
done_criteria: a @pytest.mark.milestone test asserts the fraud fixture's injection vector == the Garak-flagged vector and FAILS if a refactor decouples them (structural, not coincidence of two separate fixtures)

features.title: Posture dashboard (Streamlit) over workflow + ledger
features.description: Streamlit front-end presenting compliance posture across all three layers, rendering ledger contents from synthetic data. Survives from old KS-0401 (re-phased into Integration & demo).
done_criteria: the app launches and renders the cross-layer posture and ledger contents from synthetic data

features.title: Golden-path end-to-end demo run
features.description: A single golden-path run exercising all three layers (obligation mapping → assurance loop → transaction monitor) end to end.
done_criteria: one command drives the golden path across all three layers and produces a verifiable ledger trail

features.title: Offline fallback demo path
features.description: The demo runs without network/hosted NIM by falling back to the local backend, so the golden path works offline.
done_criteria: with the hosted backend unavailable, the demo completes via the local fallback without errors
